title: Tapping to Celebrate Daily Freedom and Joy
subtitle: A tapping practice for releasing the old belief that wealth has to feel serious, and landing fully in the freedom and joy it already supports
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 80 of a 12-week money programme. The script works with the habit of treating wealth as something sober rather than something to enjoy, and moves toward a present-tense celebration of the freedom and joy already in daily life.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from Day 80 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

practice.whenToUse: Use when wealth feels like a responsibility rather than a source of joy, or when you keep deferring enjoyment to a future point.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 80 of a 12-week money programme. Week 12 theme: live abundance as normal. Day 80 addresses the tendency to keep wealth sober and measured rather than letting it support an ordinary, joyful, free daily life.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though I once thought wealth had to feel serious, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I worried I wouldn't enjoy it fully, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I sometimes forget to celebrate, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: I notice how free my days feel.
Side of eye: I have space to create and rest.
Under eye: I can travel, give, and play.
Under nose: money supports a beautiful daily life.
Chin: I love the fun and flexibility it brings.
Collarbone: I feel grateful for this rich freedom.
Under arm: I enjoy every simple and grand moment.
Top of head: I celebrate the joyful life I've built.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: I live every day in freedom and joy.
Side of eye: I create a life that feels rich and relaxed.
Under eye: I fill my time with what matters most.
Under nose: I celebrate both big adventures and quiet hours.
Chin: I feel free to choose what each day holds.
Collarbone: I love the happiness wealth allows.
Under arm: I enjoy abundance in every moment.
Top of head: I celebrate the joyful freedom of my life.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from

The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy. The script is adapted from Day 80 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
